---
canvas:
  type: new_quiz
  title: "Beam Bending Calculations"
  published: true
  points: 15
  allowed_attempts: 1
  calculator_type: scientific
---

:::: {.question type="numeric_question" name="Max Bending Stress" points_possible="5.0"}

A rectangular beam has a section modulus of $S = 5\times 10^{4}\ \text{mm}^3$ and
carries a maximum bending moment of $M = 2\ \text{kN·m}$. What is the maximum
bending stress in MPa? (Use $\sigma = M/S$.)

::: {.answer value="40" margin="2" margin_type="percent" correct=true}
:::

::::

:::: {.question type="formula_question" name="Parametric Bending Stress" points_possible="10.0"}

A beam carries a bending moment $M = [M]\ \text{kN·m}$ with section modulus
$S = [S]\times 10^{3}\ \text{mm}^3$. What is the bending stress in MPa?

::: {.formula}
formula: M * 1000000 / (S * 1000)
margin: 2
margin_type: percent
answer_count: 5
distribution: even
:::

::: {.variable name="M"}
min: 1
max: 10
precision: 0
:::

::: {.variable name="S"}
min: 20
max: 100
precision: 0
:::

::::
